reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 6 gates passed. An attacker can trigger this via P2P network by sending a DataColumnSidecarsByRangeRequest with startSlot from epochs before max(current_epoch - 4096, FULU_FORK_EPOCH), causing the node to waste resources attempting to serve data columns from epochs it's not required to retain, or serving incorrect/partial data. The validation function validateDataColumnSidecarsByRangeRequest (L124-144) only checks startSlot < GENESIS_SLOT (L135), with a TODO comment at L134 confirming the epoch range check is unimplemented. In contrast, dataColumnSidecarsByRoot correctly implements this check at L18-23. Spec reference from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json: 'data_column_serve_range lower bound must be max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH).' Medium severity is justified as this affects individual nodes responding to malicious P2P requests, causing resource exhaustion or data inconsistency.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: data_column_serve_range lower bound must be max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH).
